title: carex alba subsp. ussuriensis
intro: Carex alba subsp. ussuriensis, also known as the Ussuri sedge, is a subspecies of sedge plant that belongs to the Carex genus. It is native to the Ussuri region in Russia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. In traditional herbal medicine, Carex alba subsp. ussuriensis is typically prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water to make a tea, which is then used to treat various health conditions.
